_note: Repo-owned workspace eval policy (plan ~1107-1117). Every value here is HUMAN-SET and has no automatic transition logic in MVP: change it by editing this file in a reviewed commit. Rich dataset promotion reads this artifact at promotion time to stamp workspace_maturity / project_category / project_impact_level metadata and to assign train/test split membership deterministically. JSON, not YAML, per decision D2 (zero-dependency repo).

project_category._note: Default example.schema.json project_category for promoted examples. overrides maps a Linear project id (preferred, stable) or exact project name to a category for that project's runs.

project_impact_level._note: Default example.schema.json project_impact_level. Same override keying as project_category.

split_assignment._note: Deterministic train/test assignment at promotion time: bucket = first 8 bytes of sha256(example_id) interpreted as an unsigned integer, modulo total_buckets; bucket < test_buckets => test, else train. Default 1/5 test (4:1 train:test). The same example id always lands in the same split, on any machine, with no stored counter. calibration and regression are NEVER hash-assigned: they require the explicit --split flag at promotion time (flag_only_splits).

human_label_regression._note: Decision D5 default for the process-change gate (step 9 consumer): block when any human-labeled example's label degrades, or when the mean human-comparable score on test-split examples drops by more than this absolute amount.
